meta.name: WHODAS 2.0 WHO Disability Assessment Schedule
meta.description: The WHODAS 2.0 is a 12-item self-report measure developed by the World Health Organization to assess health-related disability across six domains: Cognition, Mobility, Self-care, Getting along, Life activities, and Participation. It provides a standardized, cross-cultural assessment of functioning and disability regardless of medical diagnosis.
meta.author: World Health Organization (2010)
meta.reference: Ustun TB, Kostanjesek N, Chatterji S, Rehm J. Measuring health and disability: Manual for WHO Disability Assessment Schedule (WHODAS 2.0). World Health Organization. 2010.
meta.instruction: In the last 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in the following activities? Please select the most appropriate response for each item.

questions.text: Concentrating on doing something for ten minutes?
Standing for long periods such as 30 minutes?
Walking a long distance such as a kilometre?
Dealing with people you do not know?
How much of a problem did you have in doing your most important day-to-day activities well?
How much have you been emotionally affected by your health condition?
Joining in community activities in the same way as anyone else can?

ranges.label: No Disability
ranges.description: Your total score indicates no significant disability. You are functioning well across the assessed domains. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current level of functioning
Engage in health-promoting activities to preserve your well-being
Monitor any changes in your functioning over time
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

ranges.label: Mild Disability
ranges.description: Your total score indicates mild disability. Some areas of daily functioning may be affected to a limited degree. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Identify the domains where you experience the most difficulty
Consider adaptive strategies or tools to support daily activities
Discuss any concerns with your healthcare provider
Maintain physical activity appropriate to your abilities
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: Moderate Disability
ranges.description: Your total score indicates moderate disability. Several areas of daily functioning are notably affected. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consult with healthcare professionals about rehabilitation options
Explore assistive devices or environmental modifications
Seek support services for areas of greatest difficulty
Review your subscale scores to prioritize intervention areas
Consider consulting a mental health professional

ranges.label: Severe Disability
ranges.description: Your total score indicates severe disability. Many areas of daily functioning are significantly impaired and require professional support. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Seek comprehensive assessment from a multidisciplinary team
Explore available disability support services and programs
Discuss treatment and rehabilitation options with your healthcare provider
Consider applying for relevant support and assistance programs
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible

cognition.label: None
cognition.description: No significant difficulty with cognition. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

cognition.label: Mild
cognition.description: Mild difficulty with concentration and memory. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

cognition.label: Moderate
cognition.description: Moderate cognitive difficulties affecting daily tasks. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

cognition.label: Severe
cognition.description: Severe cognitive difficulties significantly impairing daily functioning. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

Maintain a structured daily routine
